skill.name: research-merge
skill.description: Processes Claude Code Web research branches by previewing findings, squash-merging approved content, organizing research documents, and optionally creating GitHub issues.
skill.summary: Merge and organize Claude Code Web research branches with review checkpoints.
skill.author: jrc1883

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-severity crypto, key, and obfuscation patterns, but review found no evidence of malicious intent, credential access, obfuscation, or weak cryptographic use. Confirmed risk comes from legitimate but powerful git, GitHub CLI, filesystem, and helper script operations that can merge content, move files, create issues, and delete remote branches.

medium_findings.title: Destructive Git Operations Require User Confirmation
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to squash-merge research branches, move files, commit changes, create GitHub issues, and delete remote branches. These operations are legitimate for the skill purpose, but mistakes can alter repository history or remove remote research branches.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly documented and include destructive branch deletion. The workflow also requires user prompts, so this is operational risk rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Helper Scripts Read and Write User-Selected Files
medium_findings.description: The included Python scripts accept file paths from command-line arguments, read research documents, and can write a merged output path. This is expected behavior, but users should run it only on intended project files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scripts directly access paths supplied by the caller. There is no network exfiltration evidence, but broad path inputs and output writes create moderate local data handling risk.

low_findings.title: Static Crypto and Key Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported certificate, key, and weak cryptography hits occur in research merge text, path variables, markdown examples, and workflow labels. No evidence found of cryptographic algorithms, private keys, or certificate material.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines shows normal research merge terminology and argument parsing, not key handling or weak crypto. The scanner likely matched substrings such as merge-related words and markdown text.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings point to Claude Code attribution links inside example commit messages. They do not send data or define a network destination for exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are static markdown links in commit message examples. No code path transmits secrets or content to those URLs.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Repository Modification Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes commands that modify local and remote repository state, including git merge, git mv, git commit, gh issue create, and git push origin --delete.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are plainly present and can change repository state. They are aligned with the skill purpose and are guarded by user decision steps.

dangerous_patterns.title: Workflow-Driven Script Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow references local Python scripts for duplicate detection, conflict detection, and finding merges. Script execution is expected, but marketplace users should understand that local files are processed.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The workflow directly names executable script steps. The scripts appear focused on local research documents and do not show obfuscation or network behavior.

content.value_statement: Research created in Claude Code Web can remain scattered across temporary branches. This skill guides review, merge, document organization, and issue creation with explicit approval steps.
actual_capabilities: Detects Claude research branches that match the expected research branch pattern.
Previews branch topics, changed documentation, commit counts, and summaries before action.
Guides squash merges into the main branch after user approval.
Moves research documents into docs/research when needed.
Creates GitHub issues from research summaries and implementation tasks.
Runs helper scripts to merge notes and detect duplicate or conflicting findings.
limitations: It is intended only for research branches, not normal feature branches.
It depends on local git and optional GitHub CLI access.
Merge conflicts still require manual review and resolution.
Branch deletion is irreversible once pushed to the remote.

use_cases.title: Process Mobile Research Sessions
use_cases.description: Merge research created from Claude Code Web into a local repository with review checkpoints.

use_cases.title: Turn Findings Into Issues
use_cases.description: Create structured GitHub issues from research summaries, source branches, and implementation tasks.

use_cases.title: Consolidate Research Notes
use_cases.description: Combine multiple research documents, remove duplicates, and identify possible conflicts before publishing.

prompt_templates.title: Find Research Branches
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of available research branches
prompt_templates.prompt: Check for Claude research branches and show me a preview of each one before taking action.

prompt_templates.title: Merge One Research Branch
prompt_templates.prompt: Process the selected research branch, squash-merge it, move research documents into docs/research, and stop before deleting the remote branch.

prompt_templates.title: Create Research Issue
prompt_templates.scenario: Convert merged findings into tracked work
prompt_templates.prompt: Merge this research branch and create a GitHub issue using the research summary, implementation tasks, and source branch details.

prompt_templates.title: Consolidate Conflicting Notes
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced merge of multiple research documents
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the research merge workflow across these notes, identify duplicate and conflicting findings, and prepare a unified research document for review.

output_examples.input: Preview available research branches before choosing an action.
output_examples.output: A table of research branches with topic, age, commit count, changed documents, and a short summary for each branch.

output_examples.input: Merge a selected branch and create an issue.
output_examples.output: A completion report listing the merged branch, moved documents, commit summary, created issue title, and whether the remote branch was deleted.

output_examples.input: Combine several research notes into one document.
output_examples.output: A merged research summary with deduplicated key findings, follow-up questions, references, and source document attribution.

best_practices: Review the branch preview and working tree status before approving a merge.
Create issues before deleting remote research branches when findings need follow-up.
Keep research documents in the expected docs/research structure for consistent results.
anti_patterns: Do not use this workflow for feature branches or release branches.
Do not approve branch deletion until merged content and issues are verified.
Do not run helper scripts on private files outside the intended research set.

faq.question: What branches should this skill process?
faq.answer: It is designed for Claude research branches and branches that contain research documentation.

faq.question: Does it merge code automatically?
faq.answer: It guides a squash merge after user approval. It should not be used for normal feature branch merges.

faq.question: Can it create GitHub issues?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create issues from research summaries and task lists when the GitHub CLI is available.

faq.question: Does it delete branches?
faq.answer: It can delete remote research branches after confirmation. Users should verify merged content first.

faq.question: What happens if there are merge conflicts?
faq.answer: The workflow should show the conflicts and require manual resolution before continuing.

faq.question: Is this safe for private research?
faq.answer: It processes local files and may create GitHub issues. Review content before publishing private findings.
